1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→"""
     3→Generate LLVM call graphs for Descent game logic files.
     4→
     5→Uses compile_commands.json to get exact flags, compiles to LLVM IR,
     6→runs opt -dot-callgraph, pipes through llvm-cxxfilt to demangle,
     7→then merges into a unified graph with porting status annotations.
     8→
     9→Usage:
    10→    python3 scripts/gen_callgraph.py [--render]
    11→
    12→Output:
    13→    callgraph/                     - per-file IR and DOT files
    14→    callgraph/unified.dot          - merged call graph with porting annotations
    15→    callgraph/unified.svg          - rendered SVG (if --render and graphviz installed)
    16→    callgraph/porting.json         - machine-readable porting status + graph data
    17→"""
    18→
    19→import json
    20→import os
    21→import re
    22→import subprocess
    23→import sys
    24→from collections import defaultdict
    25→from pathlib import Path
    26→
    27→PROJECT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
    28→BUILD_DIR = PROJECT / "build-c-ref"
    29→COMPILE_DB = BUILD_DIR / "compile_commands.json"
    30→LLVM_PREFIX = subprocess.check_output(
    31→    ["brew", "--prefix", "llvm@20"], text=True
    32→).strip()
    33→CLANG = f"{LLVM_PREFIX}/bin/clang++"
    34→OPT = f"{LLVM_PREFIX}/bin/opt"
    35→CXXFILT = f"{LLVM_PREFIX}/bin/llvm-cxxfilt"
    36→OUT_DIR = PROJECT / "callgraph"
    37→
    38→# Core game logic files we care about for the port
    39→GAME_LOGIC_FILES = [
    40→    "main_d2/ai.cpp",
    41→    "main_d2/ai2.cpp",
    42→    "main_d2/aipath.cpp",
    43→    "main_d2/physics.cpp",
    44→    "main_d2/collide.cpp",
    45→    "main_d2/fvi.cpp",
    46→    "main_d2/laser.cpp",

    50→    "main_d2/cntrlcen.cpp",

   384→    # C++ stdlib
   385→    "bool std::__1::__less<void, void>::operator",
   386→    "int const& std::__1::min[abi:ne200100]<int, std::__1::__less<void, void>>",
   387→    "int const& std::__1::min[abi:ne200100]<int>",
   388→    "int const& std::__1::max[abi:ne200100]<int, std::__1::__less<void, void>>",
   389→    "int const& std::__1::max[abi:ne200100]<int>",
   390→    "unsigned short const& std::__1::min[abi:ne200100]<unsigned short, std::__1::__less<void, void>>",
   391→    "unsigned short const& std::__1::min[abi:ne200100]<unsigned short>",
   392→    # Palette / HUD (side effects, not game logic)
   393→    "PALETTE_FLASH_ADD",
   394→}
   395→
   396→
   397→def clean_name(name):
   398→    """Strip LLVM \\x01 prefix and C++ parameter list: '\\x01_fopen(...)' -> '_fopen'"""
   399→    name = name.lstrip("\x01")
   400→    paren = name.find("(")
   401→    return name[:paren] if paren >= 0 else name
   402→
   403→
   404→def parse_compile_commands():
   405→    with open(COMPILE_DB) as f:
   406→        entries = json.load(f)
   407→    commands = {}
   408→    for entry in entries:
   409→        rel = os.path.relpath(entry["file"], PROJECT)
   410→        if rel in GAME_LOGIC_FILES:
   411→            commands[rel] = entry
   412→    return commands
   413→
   414→
   415→def extract_flags(command_str):
   416→    parts = command_str.split()
   417→    flags = []
   418→    skip_next = False
   419→    for i, p in enumerate(parts):
   420→        if skip_next:
   421→            skip_next = False
   422→            continue
   423→        if p in ("-o", "-c"):
   424→            skip_next = True
   425→            continue
   426→        if i == 0:
   427→            continue
   428→        if p.startswith("/") and p.endswith(".cpp"):
   429→            continue
   430→        flags.append(p)
   431→    return flags
   432→
   433→
   434→def compile_to_ir(commands):
   435→    ir_files = []
   436→    for rel_path, entry in commands.items():
   437→        src = PROJECT / rel_path
   438→        ir_file = OUT_DIR / (rel_path.replace("/", "_").replace(".cpp", ".ll"))
   439→
   440→        flags = extract_flags(entry["command"])
   441→        cmd = [CLANG] + flags + [
   442→            "-emit-llvm", "-S", "-Xclang", "-disable-O0-optnone",
   443→            "-o", str(ir_file), "-c", str(src),
   444→        ]
   445→
   446→        print(f"  {rel_path} -> {ir_file.name}")
   447→        result = subprocess.run(
   448→            cmd, capture_output=True, text=True,
   449→            cwd=entry.get("directory", str(PROJECT)),
   450→        )
   451→        if result.returncode != 0:
   452→            print(f"    WARNING: failed: {result.stderr[:500]}")
   453→            continue
   454→        ir_files.append(ir_file)
   455→    return ir_files
   456→
   457→
   458→def generate_demangled_dots(ir_files):
   459→    """Run opt -dot-callgraph on each IR file, demangle with llvm-cxxfilt."""
   460→    dot_files = []
   461→    for ir_file in ir_files:
   462→        cmd = [OPT, "-passes=dot-callgraph", "-disable-output", str(ir_file)]
   463→        subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, cwd=str(OUT_DIR))
   464→
   465→        raw_dot = ir_file.parent / f"{ir_file.name}.callgraph.dot"
   466→        if not raw_dot.exists():
   467→            print(f"    WARNING: no dot for {ir_file.stem}")
   468→            continue
   469→
   470→        # Demangle in-place by piping through llvm-cxxfilt
   471→        demangled_dot = OUT_DIR / f"{ir_file.stem}.callgraph.dot"
   472→        with open(raw_dot) as inp:
   473→            result = subprocess.run(
   474→                [CXXFILT], stdin=inp, capture_output=True, text=True,
   475→            )
   476→        demangled_dot.write_text(result.stdout)
   477→        if raw_dot != demangled_dot:
   478→            raw_dot.unlink()
   479→
   480→        dot_files.append((ir_file.stem, demangled_dot))
   481→        print(f"  {ir_file.stem}")
   482→    return dot_files
   483→
   484→
   485→def parse_dot_file(dot_path):
   486→    content = dot_path.read_text()
   487→    labels = {}
   488→    edges = []
   489→
   490→    for m in re.finditer(
   491→        r'(Node0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*\[.*?label\s*=\s*"\{([^}]*)\}"', content
   492→    ):
   493→        node_id, label = m.groups()
   494→        labels[node_id] = clean_name(label.strip())
   495→
   496→    for m in re.finditer(
   497→        r'(Node0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)\s*->\s*(Node0x[0-9a-fA-F]+)', content
   498→    ):
   499→        edges.append(m.groups())
   500→
   501→    return labels, edges
   502→
   503→
   504→def build_unified_graph(dot_files):
   505→    all_edges = set()
   506→    all_functions = set()
   507→    function_to_file = {}
   508→
   509→    for file_stem, dot_path in dot_files:
   510→        labels, edges = parse_dot_file(dot_path)
   511→        for src, dst in edges:
   512→            src_name = labels.get(src)
   513→            dst_name = labels.get(dst)
   514→            if src_name and dst_name:
   515→                all_edges.add((src_name, dst_name))
   516→                all_functions.add(src_name)
   517→                all_functions.add(dst_name)
   518→                if src_name not in function_to_file:
   519→                    function_to_file[src_name] = file_stem
   520→                if dst_name not in function_to_file:
   521→                    function_to_file[dst_name] = file_stem
   522→
   523→    return all_functions, all_edges, function_to_file
   524→
   525→
   526→def compute_porting_frontier(functions, edges):
   527→    """Unported, non-intrinsic, non-effect functions called directly by ported functions."""
   528→    frontier = set()
   529→    for caller, callee in edges:
   530→        if (caller in PORTED_FUNCTIONS
   531→                and callee not in PORTED_FUNCTIONS
   532→                and callee not in INTRINSICS
   533→                and callee not in EFFECT_FUNCTIONS):
   534→            frontier.add(callee)
   535→    return frontier
   536→
   537→
   538→def compute_transitive_deps(edges):
   539→    adj = defaultdict(set)
   540→    for caller, callee in edges:
   541→        adj[caller].add(callee)
   542→
   543→    cache = {}
   544→
   545→    def transitive(fn, visited=None):
   546→        if fn in cache:
   547→            return cache[fn]
   548→        if visited is None:
   549→            visited = set()
   550→        if fn in visited:
   551→            return set()
   552→        visited.add(fn)
   553→        deps = set(adj[fn])
   554→        for callee in list(adj[fn]):
   555→            deps |= transitive(callee, visited)
   556→        cache[fn] = deps
   557→        return deps
   558→
   559→    for fn in list(adj.keys()):
   560→        transitive(fn)
   561→    return cache
   562→
   563→
   564→def write_unified_dot(functions, edges, function_to_file, out_path):
   565→    frontier = compute_porting_frontier(functions, edges)
   566→
   567→    with open(out_path, "w") as f:
   568→        f.write("digraph callgraph {\n")
   569→        f.write("  rankdir=LR;\n")
   570→        f.write("  node [shape=box, fontname=monospace, fontsize=10];\n")
   571→        f.write("  edge [color=gray40];\n\n")
   572→
   573→        for fn in sorted(functions):
   574→            status = PORTED_FUNCTIONS.get(fn)
   575→            if status == "authoritative":
   576→                color, style, fillcolor = "darkgreen", "filled", "palegreen"
   577→            elif status == "ported":
   578→                color, style, fillcolor = "green", "filled", "honeydew"
   579→            elif fn in INTRINSICS:
   580→                color, style, fillcolor = "dodgerblue", "filled", "aliceblue"
   581→            elif fn in EFFECT_FUNCTIONS:
   582→                color, style, fillcolor = "orange", "filled", "lightyellow"
   583→            elif fn in frontier:
   584→                color, style, fillcolor = "red", "filled", "mistyrose"
   585→            else:
   586→                color, style, fillcolor = "gray50", "solid", "white"
   587→
   588→            file_info = function_to_file.get(fn, "")
   589→            if file_info:
   590→                file_info = file_info.replace("main_d2_", "")
   591→            label = f"{fn}\\n({file_info})" if file_info else fn
   592→
   593→            f.write(f'  "{fn}" [label="{label}", color={color}, '
   594→                    f'style={style}, fillcolor="{fillcolor}"];\n')
   595→
   596→        f.write("\n")
   597→        for caller, callee in sorted(edges):
   598→            f.write(f'  "{caller}" -> "{callee}";\n')
   599→        f.write("}\n")
   600→
   601→
   602→def write_porting_json(functions, edges, function_to_file):
   603→    frontier = compute_porting_frontier(functions, edges)
   604→    trans_deps = compute_transitive_deps(edges)
   605→
   606→    callers_map = defaultdict(set)
   607→    callees_map = defaultdict(set)
   608→    for caller, callee in edges:
   609→        callers_map[callee].add(caller)
   610→        callees_map[caller].add(callee)
   611→
   612→    n_ported = len([f for f in functions if f in PORTED_FUNCTIONS])
   613→    n_intrinsic = len([f for f in functions if f in INTRINSICS])
   614→    n_effect = len([f for f in functions if f in EFFECT_FUNCTIONS])
   615→    data = {
   616→        "summary": {
   617→            "total_functions": len(functions),
   618→            "ported": n_ported,
   619→            "intrinsic": n_intrinsic,
   620→            "effect": n_effect,
   621→            "frontier": len(frontier),
   622→            "unported": len(functions) - n_ported - n_intrinsic - n_effect - len(frontier),
   623→        },
   624→        "functions": {},
   625→        "frontier_priority": [],
   626→        "next_to_port": [],
   627→    }
   628→
   629→    def is_handled(fn):
   630→        """Check if a function is ported, an intrinsic, an effect, or otherwise handled."""
   631→        return fn in PORTED_FUNCTIONS or fn in INTRINSICS or fn in EFFECT_FUNCTIONS
   632→
   633→    for fn in sorted(functions):
   634→        if fn in PORTED_FUNCTIONS:
   635→            status = PORTED_FUNCTIONS[fn]
   636→        elif fn in INTRINSICS:
   637→            status = "intrinsic"
   638→        elif fn in EFFECT_FUNCTIONS:
   639→            status = "effect"
   640→        elif fn in frontier:
   641→            status = "frontier"
   642→        else:
   643→            status = "unported"
   644→        data["functions"][fn] = {
   645→            "status": status,
   646→            "file": function_to_file.get(fn, "external"),
   647→            "callers": sorted(callers_map.get(fn, set())),
   648→            "callees": sorted(callees_map.get(fn, set())),
   649→            "transitive_dep_count": len(trans_deps.get(fn, set())),
   650→        }
   651→
   652→    # Frontier priority: sort by fewest unported transitive deps (easiest first)
   653→    # Exclude intrinsics from unported dep count — they're already in OCaml
   654→    for fn in frontier:
   655→        fn_deps = trans_deps.get(fn, set())
   656→        real_unported = [d for d in fn_deps if not is_handled(d)]
   657→        ported_callers = [c for c in callers_map.get(fn, set()) if c in PORTED_FUNCTIONS]
   658→        data["frontier_priority"].append({
   659→            "function": fn,
   660→            "file": function_to_file.get(fn, "external").replace("main_d2_", ""),
   661→            "unported_dep_count": len(real_unported),
   662→            "unported_deps": sorted(real_unported)[:10],
   663→            "total_dep_count": len(fn_deps),
   664→            "ported_callers": sorted(ported_callers),
   665→            "all_callers": sorted(callers_map.get(fn, set())),
   666→        })
   667→
   668→    data["frontier_priority"].sort(
   669→        key=lambda x: (x["unported_dep_count"], -len(x["ported_callers"]))
   670→    )
   671→
   672→    # Next-to-port: unported functions sorted by fewest unported transitive deps
   673→    # These are candidates to port when the frontier is empty
   674→    # Filter: must have at least 1 callee (excludes external stubs/leaf nodes)
   675→    for fn in sorted(functions):
   676→        if fn in PORTED_FUNCTIONS or fn in INTRINSICS or fn in EFFECT_FUNCTIONS or fn in frontier:
   677→            continue
   678→        fn_callees = callees_map.get(fn, set())
   679→        if not fn_callees:
   680→            continue  # skip leaf/external stubs
   681→        fn_deps = trans_deps.get(fn, set())
   682→        real_unported = [d for d in fn_deps if not is_handled(d)]
   683→        data["next_to_port"].append({
   684→            "function": fn,
   685→            "file": function_to_file.get(fn, "external").replace("main_d2_", ""),
   686→            "unported_dep_count": len(real_unported),
   687→            "total_dep_count": len(fn_deps),
   688→            "callers": sorted(callers_map.get(fn, set()))[:5],
   689→            "callees": sorted(fn_callees)[:10],
   690→        })
   691→
   692→    data["next_to_port"].sort(
   693→        key=lambda x: (x["unported_dep_count"], x["total_dep_count"])
   694→    )
   695→
   696→    out_path = OUT_DIR / "porting.json"
   697→    with open(out_path, "w") as f:
   698→        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
   699→    print(f"\nWrote {out_path}")
   700→    return data
   701→
   702→
   703→def print_summary(data):
   704→    s = data["summary"]
   705→    print(f"\n{'='*60}")
   706→    print(f"PORTING STATUS")
   707→    print(f"{'='*60}")
   708→    print(f"  Total: {s['total_functions']}  Ported: {s['ported']}  "
   709→          f"Intrinsic: {s['intrinsic']}  Effect: {s['effect']}  "
   710→          f"Frontier: {s['frontier']}  Other: {s['unported']}")
   711→
   712→    print(f"\n{'='*60}")
   713→    print(f"PORTING FRONTIER (easiest first)")
   714→    print(f"{'='*60}")
   715→    for item in data["frontier_priority"][:30]:
   716→        fn = item["function"]
   717→        deps = item["unported_dep_count"]
   718→        total = item["total_dep_count"]
   719→        callers = ", ".join(item["ported_callers"][:3])
   720→        print(f"  {fn}  [{item['file']}]")
   721→        print(f"    {deps} unported deps / {total} total"
   722→              + (f"  <- {callers}" if callers else ""))
   723→
   724→    print(f"\n{'='*60}")
   725→    print(f"NEXT TO PORT (simplest first)")
   726→    print(f"{'='*60}")
   727→    for item in data["next_to_port"][:20]:
   728→        fn = item["function"]
   729→        deps = item["unported_dep_count"]
   730→        total = item["total_dep_count"]
   731→        callees = ", ".join(item["callees"][:5])
   732→        print(f"  {fn}  [{item['file']}]")
   733→        print(f"    {deps} unported deps / {total} total"
   734→              + (f"  -> {callees}" if callees else ""))
   735→
   736→
   737→def main():
   738→    render = "--render" in sys.argv
   739→    OUT_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
   740→
   741→    print("[1/5] Parsing compile_commands.json...")
   742→    commands = parse_compile_commands()
   743→    print(f"  {len(commands)}/{len(GAME_LOGIC_FILES)} files")
   744→
   745→    print("\n[2/5] Compiling to LLVM IR...")
   746→    ir_files = compile_to_ir(commands)
   747→
   748→    print(f"\n[3/5] Generating call graphs (opt + llvm-cxxfilt)...")
   749→    dot_files = generate_demangled_dots(ir_files)
   750→
   751→    print(f"\n[4/5] Building unified graph...")
   752→    functions, edges, function_to_file = build_unified_graph(dot_files)
   753→    print(f"  {len(functions)} functions, {len(edges)} edges")
   754→
   755→    write_unified_dot(functions, edges, function_to_file, OUT_DIR / "unified.dot")
   756→    data = write_porting_json(functions, edges, function_to_file)
   757→
   758→    if render:
   759→        print("\n[5/5] Rendering SVG...")
   760→        svg = OUT_DIR / "unified.svg"
   761→        r = subprocess.run(
   762→            ["dot", "-Tsvg", str(OUT_DIR / "unified.dot"), "-o", str(svg)],
   763→            capture_output=True, text=True,
   764→        )
   765→        if r.returncode == 0:
   766→            print(f"  Wrote {svg}")
   767→        else:
   768→            print(f"  Failed (install graphviz?): {r.stderr[:200]}")
   769→
   770→    print_summary(data)
   771→
   772→
   773→if __name__ == "__main__":
   774→    main()
   775→

1→(* Effect: fetch 87-int segment data array for a given segment number.
     2→   Used by FVI and find_point_seg to access segment data on demand
     3→   instead of pre-packing all segments upfront. *)
     4→type _ Effect.t += Fetch_segment_data : int -> int array Effect.t
     5→
     6→let side_to_verts =
     7→  [| [| 7; 6; 2; 3 |]
     8→   ; [| 0; 4; 7; 3 |]
     9→   ; [| 0; 1; 5; 4 |]
    10→   ; [| 2; 6; 5; 1 |]
    11→   ; [| 4; 5; 6; 7 |]
    12→   ; [| 3; 2; 1; 0 |]
    13→  |]
    14→;;
    15→
    16→let _side_is_quad = 1 [@@warning "-32"]
    17→let _side_is_tri_02 = 2 [@@warning "-32"]
    18→let _side_is_tri_13 = 3 [@@warning "-32"]
    19→let _wleft = 0 [@@warning "-32"]
    20→let wtop = 1
    21→let _wright = 2 [@@warning "-32"]
    22→let wbottom = 3
    23→let wback = 4
    24→let wfront = 5
    25→let plane_dist_tolerance = 250
    26→
    27→type vec3 = int * int * int
    28→
    29→let compute_center_point_on_side ~(v0 : vec3) ~(v1 : vec3) ~(v2 : vec3) ~(v3 : vec3)
    30→  : vec3
    31→  =
    32→  (* C uses vm_vec_add2 loop + vm_vec_scale(F1_0/4), NOT vm_vec_avg4.
    33→     fixmul rounds toward -inf for negatives, / 4 rounds toward zero. *)
    34→  let open Ox_math in
    35→  let s = vm_vec_add2 ~a:(vm_vec_add2 ~a:(vm_vec_add2 ~a:v0 ~b:v1) ~b:v2) ~b:v3 in
    36→  let sx, sy, sz = s in
    37→  let scale = 0x10000 / 4 in
    38→  fixmul ~a:sx ~b:scale, fixmul ~a:sy ~b:scale, fixmul ~a:sz ~b:scale
    39→;;
    40→
    41→let compute_segment_center
    42→      ~(v0 : vec3)
    43→      ~(v1 : vec3)
    44→      ~(v2 : vec3)
    45→      ~(v3 : vec3)
    46→      ~(v4 : vec3)
    47→      ~(v5 : vec3)
    48→      ~(v6 : vec3)
    49→      ~(v7 : vec3)
    50→  : vec3
    51→  =
    52→  let open Ox_math in
    53→  let s =
    54→    vm_vec_add2
    55→      ~a:
    56→        (vm_vec_add2
    57→           ~a:
    58→             (vm_vec_add2
    59→                ~a:
    60→                  (vm_vec_add2
    61→                     ~a:
    62→                       (vm_vec_add2
    63→                          ~a:(vm_vec_add2 ~a:(vm_vec_add2 ~a:v0 ~b:v1) ~b:v2)
    64→                          ~b:v3)
    65→                     ~b:v4)
    66→                ~b:v5)
    67→           ~b:v6)
    68→      ~b:v7
    69→  in
    70→  let sx, sy, sz = s in
    71→  let scale = 0x10000 / 8 in
    72→  Ox_math.fixmul ~a:sx ~b:scale, Ox_math.fixmul ~a:sy ~b:scale, Ox_math.fixmul ~a:sz ~b:scale
    73→;;
    74→
    75→let get_verts_for_normal ~va ~vb ~vc ~vd =
    76→  let v = [| va; vb; vc; vd |] in
    77→  let w = [| 0; 1; 2; 3 |] in
    78→  for i = 1 to 3 do
    79→    for j = 0 to i - 1 do
    80→      if v.(j) > v.(i)
    81→      then (
    82→        let t = v.(j) in
    83→        v.(j) <- v.(i);
    84→        v.(i) <- t;
    85→        let t = w.(j) in
    86→        w.(j) <- w.(i);
    87→        w.(i) <- t)
    88→    done
    89→  done;
    90→  let negate_flag =
    91→    if (w.(0) + 3) mod 4 = w.(1) || (w.(1) + 3) mod 4 = w.(2) then 1 else 0
    92→  in
    93→  v.(0), v.(1), v.(2), v.(3), negate_flag
    94→;;
    95→
    96→let create_abs_vertex_lists ~side_type ~(seg_verts : int array) ~sidenum =
    97→  let sv = side_to_verts.(sidenum) in
    98→  match side_type with
    99→  | 1 ->
   100→    (* SIDE_IS_QUAD *)
   101→    let vertices =
   102→      [| seg_verts.(sv.(0))
   103→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(1))
   104→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(2))
   105→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(3))
   106→       ; 0
   107→       ; 0
   108→      |]
   109→    in
   110→    1, vertices
   111→  | 2 ->
   112→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_02 *)
   113→    let vertices =
   114→      [| seg_verts.(sv.(0))
   115→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(1))
   116→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(2))
   117→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(2))
   118→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(3))
   119→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(0))
   120→      |]
   121→    in
   122→    2, vertices
   123→  | 3 ->
   124→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_13 *)
   125→    let vertices =
   126→      [| seg_verts.(sv.(3))
   127→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(0))
   128→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(1))
   129→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(1))
   130→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(2))
   131→       ; seg_verts.(sv.(3))
   132→      |]
   133→    in
   134→    2, vertices
   135→  | _ -> failwith (Printf.sprintf "Illegal side type %d in create_abs_vertex_lists (sidenum=%d)" side_type sidenum)
   136→;;
   137→
   138→(* Lookup a vertex position by absolute vertex index.
   139→   seg_verts maps relative index 0..7 to absolute vertex indices.
   140→   seg_vert_positions.(i) is the position of seg_verts.(i). *)
   141→let lookup_vpos ~(seg_verts : int array) ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array) ~abs_idx =
   142→  let rec find i =
   143→    if i >= 8
   144→    then 0, 0, 0 (* should not happen *)
   145→    else if seg_verts.(i) = abs_idx
   146→    then seg_vert_positions.(i)
   147→    else find (i + 1)
   148→  in
   149→  find 0
   150→;;
   151→
   152→let get_seg_masks
   153→      ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz)
   154→      ~rad
   155→      ~(seg_verts : int array)
   156→      ~(side_types : int array)
   157→      ~(normals : vec3 array)
   158→      ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array)
   159→  =
   160→  let vpos abs_idx = lookup_vpos ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions ~abs_idx in
   161→  let facemask = ref 0 in
   162→  let sidemask = ref 0 in
   163→  let centermask = ref 0 in
   164→  let facebit = ref 1 in
   165→  let sidebit = ref 1 in
   166→  for sn = 0 to 5 do
   167→    let side_type = side_types.(sn) in
   168→    let n0 = normals.(sn * 2) in
   169→    let n1 = normals.((sn * 2) + 1) in
   170→    let num_faces, vertex_list =
   171→      create_abs_vertex_lists ~side_type ~seg_verts ~sidenum:sn
   172→    in
   173→    if num_faces = 2
   174→    then (
   175→      let vertnum = min vertex_list.(0) vertex_list.(2) in
   176→      let planep = vpos vertnum in
   177→      let dist =
   178→        if vertex_list.(4) < vertex_list.(1)
   179→        then Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(vpos vertex_list.(4)) ~norm:n0 ~planep
   180→        else Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(vpos vertex_list.(1)) ~norm:n1 ~planep
   181→      in
   182→      let side_pokes_out = dist > plane_dist_tolerance in
   183→      let side_count = ref 0 in
   184→      let center_count = ref 0 in
   185→      for fn = 0 to 1 do
   186→        let norm = if fn = 0 then n0 else n1 in
   187→        let d = Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz) ~norm ~planep in
   188→        if d < -plane_dist_tolerance then incr center_count;
   189→        if d - rad < -plane_dist_tolerance
   190→        then (
   191→          facemask := !facemask lor !facebit;
   192→          incr side_count);
   193→        facebit := !facebit lsl 1
   194→      done;
   195→      if not side_pokes_out
   196→      then (
   197→        if !side_count = 2 then sidemask := !sidemask lor !sidebit;
   198→        if !center_count = 2 then centermask := !centermask lor !sidebit)
   199→      else (
   200→        if !side_count > 0 then sidemask := !sidemask lor !sidebit;
   201→        if !center_count > 0 then centermask := !centermask lor !sidebit))
   202→    else (
   203→      let vertnum = ref vertex_list.(0) in
   204→      for i = 1 to 3 do
   205→        if vertex_list.(i) < !vertnum then vertnum := vertex_list.(i)
   206→      done;
   207→      let d =
   208→        Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz) ~norm:n0 ~planep:(vpos !vertnum)
   209→      in
   210→      if d < -plane_dist_tolerance then centermask := !centermask lor !sidebit;
   211→      if d - rad < -plane_dist_tolerance
   212→      then (
   213→        facemask := !facemask lor !facebit;
   214→        sidemask := !sidemask lor !sidebit);
   215→      facebit := !facebit lsl 2);
   216→    sidebit := !sidebit lsl 1
   217→  done;
   218→  !facemask, !sidemask, !centermask
   219→;;
   220→
   221→let get_side_dists
   222→      ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz)
   223→      ~(seg_verts : int array)
   224→      ~(side_types : int array)
   225→      ~(normals : vec3 array)
   226→      ~(seg_vert_positions : vec3 array)
   227→  =
   228→  let vpos abs_idx = lookup_vpos ~seg_verts ~seg_vert_positions ~abs_idx in
   229→  let mask = ref 0 in
   230→  let side_dists = Array.create ~len:6 0 in
   231→  let facebit = ref 1 in
   232→  let sidebit = ref 1 in
   233→  for sn = 0 to 5 do
   234→    let side_type = side_types.(sn) in
   235→    let n0 = normals.(sn * 2) in
   236→    let n1 = normals.((sn * 2) + 1) in
   237→    let num_faces, vertex_list =
   238→      create_abs_vertex_lists ~side_type ~seg_verts ~sidenum:sn
   239→    in
   240→    side_dists.(sn) <- 0;
   241→    if num_faces = 2
   242→    then (
   243→      let vertnum = min vertex_list.(0) vertex_list.(2) in
   244→      let planep = vpos vertnum in
   245→      let dist =
   246→        if vertex_list.(4) < vertex_list.(1)
   247→        then Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(vpos vertex_list.(4)) ~norm:n0 ~planep
   248→        else Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(vpos vertex_list.(1)) ~norm:n1 ~planep
   249→      in
   250→      let side_pokes_out = dist > plane_dist_tolerance in
   251→      let center_count = ref 0 in
   252→      for fn = 0 to 1 do
   253→        let norm = if fn = 0 then n0 else n1 in
   254→        let d = Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz) ~norm ~planep in
   255→        if d < -plane_dist_tolerance
   256→        then (
   257→          incr center_count;
   258→          side_dists.(sn) <- side_dists.(sn) + d);
   259→        facebit := !facebit lsl 1
   260→      done;
   261→      if not side_pokes_out
   262→      then (
   263→        if !center_count = 2
   264→        then (
   265→          mask := !mask lor !sidebit;
   266→          side_dists.(sn) <- side_dists.(sn) / 2))
   267→      else if !center_count > 0
   268→      then (
   269→        mask := !mask lor !sidebit;
   270→        if !center_count = 2 then side_dists.(sn) <- side_dists.(sn) / 2))
   271→    else (
   272→      let vertnum = ref vertex_list.(0) in
   273→      for i = 1 to 3 do
   274→        if vertex_list.(i) < !vertnum then vertnum := vertex_list.(i)
   275→      done;
   276→      let d =
   277→        Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz) ~norm:n0 ~planep:(vpos !vertnum)
   278→      in
   279→      if d < -plane_dist_tolerance
   280→      then (
   281→        mask := !mask lor !sidebit;
   282→        side_dists.(sn) <- d);
   283→      facebit := !facebit lsl 2);
   284→    sidebit := !sidebit lsl 1
   285→  done;
   286→  !mask, side_dists
   287→;;
   288→
   289→let extract_vector_from_segment ~(verts : vec3 array) ~start_side ~end_side =
   290→  let sv_start = side_to_verts.(start_side) in
   291→  let sv_end = side_to_verts.(end_side) in
   292→  let vs = ref (0, 0, 0) in
   293→  let ve = ref (0, 0, 0) in
   294→  for i = 0 to 3 do
   295→    vs := Ox_math.vm_vec_add2 ~a:!vs ~b:verts.(sv_start.(i));
   296→    ve := Ox_math.vm_vec_add2 ~a:!ve ~b:verts.(sv_end.(i))
   297→  done;
   298→  let diff = Ox_math.vm_vec_sub ~a:!ve ~b:!vs in
   299→  Ox_math.vm_vec_scale ~v:diff ~k:(0x10000 / 4)
   300→;;
   301→
   302→let extract_orient_from_segment (verts : vec3 array) =
   303→  let fvec = extract_vector_from_segment ~verts ~start_side:wfront ~end_side:wback in
   304→  let uvec = extract_vector_from_segment ~verts ~start_side:wbottom ~end_side:wtop in
   305→  Ox_math.vm_vector_2_matrix ~fvec ~uvec:(Some uvec) ~rvec:None
   306→;;
   307→
   308→let max_sides_per_segment = 6
   309→
   310→(* find_connect_side: given a segment's children array and a base segment number,
   311→   return the side index that connects to the base segment, or -1 if none. *)
   312→let find_connect_side ~(children : int array) ~base_seg_num =
   313→  let rec loop s =
   314→    if s >= max_sides_per_segment
   315→    then -1
   316→    else if children.(s) = base_seg_num
   317→    then s
   318→    else loop (s + 1)
   319→  in
   320→  loop 0
   321→;;
   322→
   323→(* --- find_connected_distance ----------------------------------------- *)
   324→(* BFS to find distance between two points via segment connectivity.
   325→   Packed array layout:
   326→   Header (12 ints):
   327→     [0..2]  p0.x, p0.y, p0.z
   328→     [3]     seg0
   329→     [4..6]  p1.x, p1.y, p1.z
   330→     [7]     seg1
   331→     [8]     max_depth (-1 = unlimited)
   332→     [9]     wid_flag
   333→     [10]    n_segments
   334→     [11]    check_wid_on_adjacency (0=D1 skip, 1=D2 check)
   335→
   336→   Per-segment (15 ints each):
   337→     [0..5]   children[0..5]
   338→     [6..11]  wid[0..5] — pre-evaluated WALL_IS_DOORWAY results
   339→     [12..14] center.x, center.y, center.z — pre-computed segment center
   340→
   341→   Returns: [| distance (fix) or -1; connected_segment_distance |] *)
   342→
   343→let max_loc_point_segs = 64
   344→
   345→let find_connected_distance (arr : int array) =
   346→  let p0 = arr.(0), arr.(1), arr.(2) in
   347→  let seg0 = arr.(3) in
   348→  let p1 = arr.(4), arr.(5), arr.(6) in
   349→  let seg1 = arr.(7) in
   350→  let max_depth_raw = arr.(8) in
   351→  let wid_flag = arr.(9) in
   352→  let n_segments = arr.(10) in
   353→  let check_wid_adj = arr.(11) in
   354→  let header = 12 in
   355→  let per_seg = 15 in
   356→  let max_depth =
   357→    if max_depth_raw > max_loc_point_segs - 2
   358→    then max_loc_point_segs - 2
   359→    else max_depth_raw
   360→  in
   361→  let seg_children s i = arr.(header + (s * per_seg) + i) in
   362→  let seg_wid s i = arr.(header + (s * per_seg) + 6 + i) in
   363→  let seg_center s =
   364→    let base = header + (s * per_seg) + 12 in
   365→    arr.(base), arr.(base + 1), arr.(base + 2)
   366→  in
   367→  (* Quick return: same segment *)
   368→  if seg0 = seg1
   369→  then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; 0 |]
   370→  else (
   371→    (* Adjacency check *)
   372→    let conn_side = ref (-1) in
   373→    for side = 0 to 5 do
   374→      if
   375→        !conn_side = -1 && seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments && seg_children seg0 side = seg1
   376→      then conn_side := side
   377→    done;
   378→    if
   379→      !conn_side <> -1
   380→      && (check_wid_adj = 0
   381→          || (seg1 >= 0 && seg1 < n_segments && seg_wid seg1 !conn_side land wid_flag <> 0)
   382→         )
   383→    then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; 1 |]
   384→    else (
   385→      (* BFS *)
   386→      let visited = Array.create ~len:n_segments false in
   387→      let q_start = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
   388→      let q_end = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
   389→      let q_depth = Array.create ~len:n_segments 0 in
   390→      let qtail = ref 0 in
   391→      let qhead = ref 0 in
   392→      let cur_seg = ref seg0 in
   393→      let cur_depth = ref 0 in
   394→      let found = ref false in
   395→      let failed = ref false in
   396→      if seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments then visited.(seg0) <- true;
   397→      while !cur_seg <> seg1 && not !failed do
   398→        let cs = !cur_seg in
   399→        if cs < 0 || cs >= n_segments
   400→        then failed := true
   401→        else (
   402→          for sidenum = 0 to 5 do
   403→            if (not !failed) && seg_wid cs sidenum land wid_flag <> 0
   404→            then (
   405→              let child = seg_children cs sidenum in
   406→              if child >= 0 && child < n_segments && not visited.(child)
   407→              then (
   408→                let qt = !qtail in
   409→                q_start.(qt) <- cs;
   410→                q_end.(qt) <- child;
   411→                visited.(child) <- true;
   412→                q_depth.(qt) <- !cur_depth + 1;
   413→                qtail := qt + 1;
   414→                if max_depth <> -1 && !cur_depth + 1 = max_depth
   415→                then failed := true
   416→                else if max_depth = -1 && child = seg1
   417→                then (
   418→                  found := true;
   419→                  cur_seg := seg1)))
   420→          done;
   421→          if (not !found) && not !failed
   422→          then
   423→            if !qhead >= !qtail
   424→            then failed := true
   425→            else (
   426→              cur_seg := q_end.(!qhead);
   427→              cur_depth := q_depth.(!qhead);
   428→              qhead := !qhead + 1))
   429→      done;
   430→      if !failed
   431→      then [| -1; 1000 |]
   432→      else (
   433→        (* Path reconstruction: trace back from seg1 to seg0 *)
   434→        let path_centers = Array.create ~len:max_loc_point_segs (0, 0, 0) in
   435→        let num_points = ref 0 in
   436→        (* Find last queue entry ending at seg1 *)
   437→        let qt = ref (!qtail - 1) in
   438→        while !qt >= 0 && q_end.(!qt) <> seg1 do
   439→          qt := !qt - 1
   440→        done;
   441→        if !qt < 0
   442→        then [| -1; 1000 |]
   443→        else (
   444→          (* Walk backwards through BFS tree *)
   445→          let done_ = ref false in
   446→          while !qt >= 0 && not !done_ do
   447→            let this_seg = q_end.(!qt) in
   448→            let parent_seg = q_start.(!qt) in
   449→            if !num_points < max_loc_point_segs
   450→            then (
   451→              path_centers.(!num_points) <- seg_center this_seg;
   452→              num_points := !num_points + 1);
   453→            if parent_seg = seg0
   454→            then done_ := true
   455→            else (
   456→              qt := !qt - 1;
   457→              while !qt >= 0 && q_end.(!qt) <> parent_seg do
   458→                qt := !qt - 1
   459→              done;
   460→              if !qt < 0 then done_ := true)
   461→          done;
   462→          if !qt < 0 && not !done_
   463→          then [| -1; 1000 |]
   464→          else (
   465→            (* Add seg0 center *)
   466→            if !num_points < max_loc_point_segs && seg0 >= 0 && seg0 < n_segments
   467→            then (
   468→              path_centers.(!num_points) <- seg_center seg0;
   469→              num_points := !num_points + 1);
   470→            let np = !num_points in
   471→            if np = 1
   472→            then [| Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:p1; np |]
   473→            else (
   474→              (* Sum distances along path:
   475→                 p1 -> path_centers[1] -> ... -> path_centers[np-2] -> p0 *)
   476→              let dist = ref 0 in
   477→              dist
   478→              := Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p1 ~b:path_centers.(1)
   479→                 + Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:p0 ~b:path_centers.(np - 2);
   480→              for i = 1 to np - 3 do
   481→                dist
   482→                := !dist
   483→                   + Ox_math.vm_vec_dist_quick ~a:path_centers.(i) ~b:path_centers.(i + 1)
   484→              done;
   485→              [| !dist; np |]))))))
   486→;;
   487→
   488→(* --- find_connected_distance_segments --------------------------------- *)
   489→(* Thin wrapper: compute segment centers from per-segment data,
   490→   then delegate to find_connected_distance.
   491→   Packed array layout:
   492→   Header (6 ints):
   493→     [0] seg0  [1] seg1  [2] depth  [3] wid_flag
   494→     [4] n_segments  [5] check_wid_on_adjacency
   495→   Per-segment (15 ints each, same as find_connected_distance):
   496→     [0..5]   children[0..5]
   497→     [6..11]  wid[0..5]
   498→     [12..14] center.x, center.y, center.z
   499→   Returns: [| distance (fix) or -1; connected_segment_distance |] *)
   500→let find_connected_distance_segments (arr : int array) =
   501→  let seg0 = arr.(0) in
   502→  let seg1 = arr.(1) in
   503→  let depth = arr.(2) in
   504→  let wid_flag = arr.(3) in
   505→  let n_segs = arr.(4) in
   506→  let check_wid_adj = arr.(5) in
   507→  let hdr = 6 in
   508→  let per_seg = 15 in
   509→  (* Extract pre-computed centers for seg0 and seg1 *)
   510→  let base0 = hdr + seg0 * per_seg + 12 in
   511→  let p0x = arr.(base0) in
   512→  let p0y = arr.(base0 + 1) in
   513→  let p0z = arr.(base0 + 2) in
   514→  let base1 = hdr + seg1 * per_seg + 12 in
   515→  let p1x = arr.(base1) in
   516→  let p1y = arr.(base1 + 1) in
   517→  let p1z = arr.(base1 + 2) in
   518→  (* Build find_connected_distance packed array: 12-int header + per-seg data *)
   519→  let fcd_len = 12 + n_segs * per_seg in
   520→  let fcd = Array.create ~len:fcd_len 0 in
   521→  fcd.(0) <- p0x; fcd.(1) <- p0y; fcd.(2) <- p0z;
   522→  fcd.(3) <- seg0;
   523→  fcd.(4) <- p1x; fcd.(5) <- p1y; fcd.(6) <- p1z;
   524→  fcd.(7) <- seg1;
   525→  fcd.(8) <- depth;
   526→  fcd.(9) <- wid_flag;
   527→  fcd.(10) <- n_segs;
   528→  fcd.(11) <- check_wid_adj;
   529→  Array.blit ~src:arr ~src_pos:hdr ~dst:fcd ~dst_pos:12 ~len:(n_segs * per_seg);
   530→  find_connected_distance fcd
   531→;;
   532→
   533→let matrix_precision = 9
   534→let matrix_max = 0x7f
   535→let relpos_precision = 10
   536→let vel_precision = 12
   537→
   538→let convert_to_byte f =
   539→  if f >= 0x00010000
   540→  then matrix_max
   541→  else if f <= -0x00010000
   542→  then -matrix_max
   543→  else f asr matrix_precision
   544→;;
   545→
   546→(* create_shortpos: compress an object's orient/pos/vel into a shortpos.
   547→   orient: 9 ints (rx,ux,fx,ry,uy,fy,rz,uz,fz)
   548→   pos: (x,y,z), vel: (x,y,z), seg_vert0: (x,y,z) position of segment vertex 0
   549→   segnum: object's segment number
   550→   Returns: (bytemat[9], xo, yo, zo, segment, velx, vely, velz) *)
   551→let create_shortpos
   552→      ~(orient : int array)
   553→      ~(pos : vec3)
   554→      ~(vel : vec3)
   555→      ~(seg_vert0 : vec3)
   556→      ~segnum
   557→  =
   558→  let bytemat = Array.init 9 ~f:(fun i -> convert_to_byte orient.(i)) in
   559→  let px, py, pz = pos in
   560→  let v0x, v0y, v0z = seg_vert0 in
   561→  let xo = (px - v0x) asr relpos_precision in
   562→  let yo = (py - v0y) asr relpos_precision in
   563→  let zo = (pz - v0z) asr relpos_precision in
   564→  let vx, vy, vz = vel in
   565→  let velx = vx asr vel_precision in
   566→  let vely = vy asr vel_precision in
   567→  let velz = vz asr vel_precision in
   568→  bytemat, xo, yo, zo, segnum, velx, vely, velz
   569→;;
   570→
   571→(* extract_shortpos: decompress a shortpos back to orient/pos/vel.
   572→   bytemat: 9 ints, xo/yo/zo: relative position shorts,
   573→   seg_vert0: (x,y,z), velx/vely/velz: velocity shorts
   574→   Returns: (orient[9], pos(x,y,z), vel(x,y,z)) *)
   575→let extract_shortpos
   576→      ~(bytemat : int array)
   577→      ~xo
   578→      ~yo
   579→      ~zo
   580→      ~(seg_vert0 : vec3)
   581→      ~velx
   582→      ~vely
   583→      ~velz
   584→  =
   585→  let orient = Array.init 9 ~f:(fun i -> bytemat.(i) lsl matrix_precision) in
   586→  let v0x, v0y, v0z = seg_vert0 in
   587→  let pos =
   588→    ( (xo lsl relpos_precision) + v0x
   589→    , (yo lsl relpos_precision) + v0y
   590→    , (zo lsl relpos_precision) + v0z )
   591→  in
   592→  let vel = velx lsl vel_precision, vely lsl vel_precision, velz lsl vel_precision in
   593→  orient, pos, vel
   594→;;
   595→
   596→(* --- create_walls_on_side --------------------------------------------- *)
   597→(* Determine side type (quad or triangulated) and compute face normals.
   598→   Combines C functions: create_walls_on_side, add_side_as_quad, add_side_as_2_triangles.
   599→   v0..v3: 4 vertex positions (as vec3), vi0..vi3: their absolute vertex indices,
   600→   has_child: whether there's a child segment on this side.
   601→   Returns (side_type, normal0, normal1). *)
   602→
   603→let side_is_quad = 1
   604→let side_is_tri_02 = 2
   605→let side_is_tri_13 = 3
   606→let plane_dist_tolerance = 250
   607→let vec_negate (x, y, z) = -x, -y, -z
   608→
   609→(* Helper: look up vertex position by absolute index from our 4 known vertices *)
   610→let make_pos_lookup ~v0 ~vi0 ~v1 ~vi1 ~v2 ~vi2 ~v3 ~vi3 =
   611→  fun vi ->
   612→  if vi = vi0
   613→  then v0
   614→  else if vi = vi1
   615→  then v1
   616→  else if vi = vi2
   617→  then v2
   618→  else if vi = vi3
   619→  then v3
   620→  else failwith "create_walls_on_side: unknown vertex index"
   621→;;
   622→
   623→(* add_side_as_2_triangles logic: determine triangulation and compute normals *)
   624→let triangulate_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child =
   625→  let pos_of = make_pos_lookup ~v0 ~vi0 ~v1 ~vi1 ~v2 ~vi2 ~v3 ~vi3 in
   626→  if not has_child
   627→  then (
   628→    (* Non-wall case: use dot product to choose triangulation *)
   629→    let norm = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v0 ~p1:v1 ~p2:v2 in
   630→    let vec_13 = Ox_math.vm_vec_sub ~a:v3 ~b:v1 in
   631→    let dot = Ox_math.vm_vec_dotprod ~a:norm ~b:vec_13 in
   632→    if dot >= 0
   633→    then (
   634→      let n0 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v0 ~p1:v1 ~p2:v2 in
   635→      let n1 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v0 ~p1:v2 ~p2:v3 in
   636→      side_is_tri_02, n0, n1)
   637→    else (
   638→      let n0 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v0 ~p1:v1 ~p2:v3 in
   639→      let n1 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:v1 ~p1:v2 ~p2:v3 in
   640→      side_is_tri_13, n0, n1))
   641→  else (
   642→    (* Wall case: use get_verts_for_normal for consistent normals *)
   643→    let vs0, _vs1, _vs2, _vs3, _nf =
   644→      get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi1 ~vc:vi2 ~vd:vi3
   645→    in
   646→    if vs0 = vi0 || vs0 = vi2
   647→    then (
   648→      (* TRI_02 *)
   649→      let s0, s1, s2, _, nf0 = get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi1 ~vc:vi2 ~vd:32767 in
   650→      let n0 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of s0) ~p1:(pos_of s1) ~p2:(pos_of s2) in
   651→      let n0 = if nf0 <> 0 then vec_negate n0 else n0 in
   652→      let s0, s1, s2, _, nf1 = get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi2 ~vc:vi3 ~vd:32767 in
   653→      let n1 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of s0) ~p1:(pos_of s1) ~p2:(pos_of s2) in
   654→      let n1 = if nf1 <> 0 then vec_negate n1 else n1 in
   655→      side_is_tri_02, n0, n1)
   656→    else (
   657→      (* TRI_13 *)
   658→      let s0, s1, s2, _, nf0 = get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi1 ~vc:vi3 ~vd:32767 in
   659→      let n0 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of s0) ~p1:(pos_of s1) ~p2:(pos_of s2) in
   660→      let n0 = if nf0 <> 0 then vec_negate n0 else n0 in
   661→      let s0, s1, s2, _, nf1 = get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi1 ~vb:vi2 ~vc:vi3 ~vd:32767 in
   662→      let n1 = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of s0) ~p1:(pos_of s1) ~p2:(pos_of s2) in
   663→      let n1 = if nf1 <> 0 then vec_negate n1 else n1 in
   664→      side_is_tri_13, n0, n1))
   665→;;
   666→
   667→let create_walls_on_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child =
   668→  let pos_of = make_pos_lookup ~v0 ~vi0 ~v1 ~vi1 ~v2 ~vi2 ~v3 ~vi3 in
   669→  (* Step 1: Sort vertices, compute plane normal and planarity *)
   670→  let vm0, vm1, vm2, vm3, negate_flag =
   671→    get_verts_for_normal ~va:vi0 ~vb:vi1 ~vc:vi2 ~vd:vi3
   672→  in
   673→  let vn = Ox_math.vm_vec_normal ~p0:(pos_of vm0) ~p1:(pos_of vm1) ~p2:(pos_of vm2) in
   674→  let dist =
   675→    abs (Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(pos_of vm3) ~norm:vn ~planep:(pos_of vm0))
   676→  in
   677→  let vn = if negate_flag <> 0 then vec_negate vn else vn in
   678→  (* Step 2: If planar, return as quad *)
   679→  if dist <= plane_dist_tolerance
   680→  then side_is_quad, vn, vn
   681→  else (
   682→    (* Step 3: Triangulate *)
   683→    let side_type, n0, n1 =
   684→      triangulate_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child
   685→    in
   686→    (* Step 4: De-triangulation check *)
   687→    (* Build vertex list for the computed triangulation *)
   688→    let vl =
   689→      if side_type = side_is_tri_02
   690→      then [| vi0; vi1; vi2; vi2; vi3; vi0 |]
   691→      else [| vi3; vi0; vi1; vi1; vi2; vi3 |]
   692→    in
   693→    let vertnum = min vl.(0) vl.(2) in
   694→    let dist0 =
   695→      Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(pos_of vl.(1)) ~norm:n1 ~planep:(pos_of vertnum)
   696→    in
   697→    let dist1 =
   698→      Ox_math.vm_dist_to_plane ~checkp:(pos_of vl.(4)) ~norm:n0 ~planep:(pos_of vertnum)
   699→    in
   700→    let sign v =
   701→      if v > plane_dist_tolerance
   702→      then 1
   703→      else if v < -(plane_dist_tolerance + 1)
   704→      then -1
   705→      else 0
   706→    in
   707→    let s0 = sign dist0 in
   708→    let s1 = sign dist1 in
   709→    if s0 = 0 || s1 = 0 || s0 <> s1
   710→    then (* De-triangulate back to quad *)
   711→      side_is_quad, vn, vn
   712→    else side_type, n0, n1)
   713→;;
   714→
   715→(* --- check_norms ------------------------------------------------------ *)
   716→(* Check if two face normals are consistent (should be negations of each other).
   717→   Returns 1 if mismatch, 0 if OK. *)
   718→let check_norms ~(n0 : vec3) ~(n1 : vec3) =
   719→  let n0x, n0y, n0z = n0 in
   720→  let n1x, n1y, n1z = n1 in
   721→  if n0x <> -n1x || n0y <> -n1y || n0z <> -n1z then 1 else 0
   722→;;
   723→
   724→(* --- create_all_vertex_lists ------------------------------------------ *)
   725→(* Like create_abs_vertex_lists but returns relative vertex indices (0..7 within segment)
   726→   instead of absolute vertex indices. Takes side_type and sidenum. *)
   727→let create_all_vertex_lists ~side_type ~sidenum =
   728→  let sv = side_to_verts.(sidenum) in
   729→  match side_type with
   730→  | 1 ->
   731→    (* SIDE_IS_QUAD *)
   732→    1, [| sv.(0); sv.(1); sv.(2); sv.(3); 0; 0 |]
   733→  | 2 ->
   734→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_02 *)
   735→    2, [| sv.(0); sv.(1); sv.(2); sv.(2); sv.(3); sv.(0) |]
   736→  | 3 ->
   737→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_13 *)
   738→    2, [| sv.(3); sv.(0); sv.(1); sv.(1); sv.(2); sv.(3) |]
   739→  | _ -> failwith (Printf.sprintf "Illegal side type %d" side_type)
   740→;;
   741→
   742→(* --- create_all_vertnum_lists ----------------------------------------- *)
   743→(* Returns vertex number indices (0..3 within side) for each face of a side.
   744→   Only depends on side_type. *)
   745→let create_all_vertnum_lists ~side_type =
   746→  match side_type with
   747→  | 1 ->
   748→    (* SIDE_IS_QUAD *)
   749→    1, [| 0; 1; 2; 3; 0; 0 |]
   750→  | 2 ->
   751→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_02 *)
   752→    2, [| 0; 1; 2; 2; 3; 0 |]
   753→  | 3 ->
   754→    (* SIDE_IS_TRI_13 *)
   755→    2, [| 3; 0; 1; 1; 2; 3 |]
   756→  | _ -> failwith (Printf.sprintf "Illegal side type %d" side_type)
   757→;;
   758→
   759→(* --- trace_segs + find_point_seg ------------------------------------- *)
   760→(* Packed array layout (same 80-int-per-segment as sphere_intersects_wall):
   761→   Header (6 ints):
   762→     [0..2] p.x, p.y, p.z
   763→     [3]    segnum (hint, -1 if unknown)
   764→     [4]    n_segments
   765→     [5]    doing_lighting_hack (D1: always 0; D2: runtime flag)
   766→
   767→   Per-segment (80 ints each):
   768→     [0..5]   children[0..5]
   769→     [6..11]  side_types[0..5]
   770→     [12..19] seg_verts[0..7]
   771→     [20..55] normals: side 0..5, normals[0].xyz then normals[1].xyz
   772→     [56..79] vertex_positions: vert 0..7, xyz
   773→
   774→   Returns segment number or -1. *)
   775→
   776→let find_point_seg (arr : int array) =
   777→  let px = arr.(0) in
   778→  let py = arr.(1) in
   779→  let pz = arr.(2) in
   780→  let segnum = arr.(3) in
   781→  let n_segments = arr.(4) in
   782→  let doing_lighting_hack = arr.(5) in
   783→  let header = 6 in
   784→  let per_seg = 80 in
   785→  let get_seg_base s = header + (s * per_seg) in
   786→  let unpack_seg s =
   787→    let base = get_seg_base s in
   788→    let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(base + i)) in
   789→    let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(base + 6 + i)) in
   790→    let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(base + 12 + i)) in
   791→    let normals =
   792→      Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
   793→        let off = base + 20 + (i * 3) in
   794→        arr.(off), arr.(off + 1), arr.(off + 2))
   795→    in
   796→    let seg_vert_positions =
   797→      Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   798→        let off = base + 56 + (i * 3) in
   799→        arr.(off), arr.(off + 1), arr.(off + 2))
   800→    in
   801→    children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions
   802→  in
   803→  (* trace_segs: recursively find segment containing point by following
   804→     the child side with the most-negative distance. *)
   805→  let rec trace s iterations =
   806→    if s < 0 || s >= n_segments
   807→    then -1
   808→    else if iterations > 1024
   809→    then s
   810→    else (
   811→      let children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = unpack_seg s in
   812→      let centermask, side_dists =
   813→        get_side_dists
   814→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   815→          ~seg_verts
   816→          ~side_types
   817→          ~normals
   818→          ~seg_vert_positions
   819→      in
   820→      if centermask = 0
   821→      then s
   822→      else (
   823→        (* Make a mutable copy of side_dists so we can zero out tried sides *)
   824→        let sd = Array.copy side_dists in
   825→        let result = ref (-1) in
   826→        let continue_ = ref true in
   827→        while !continue_ do
   828→          let biggest_side = ref (-1) in
   829→          let biggest_val = ref 0 in
   830→          let bit = ref 1 in
   831→          for sn = 0 to 5 do
   832→            if centermask land !bit <> 0 && children.(sn) > -1 && sd.(sn) < !biggest_val
   833→            then (
   834→              biggest_val := sd.(sn);
   835→              biggest_side := sn);
   836→            bit := !bit lsl 1
   837→          done;
   838→          if !biggest_side <> -1
   839→          then (
   840→            sd.(!biggest_side) <- 0;
   841→            let check = trace children.(!biggest_side) (iterations + 1) in
   842→            if check <> -1
   843→            then (
   844→              result := check;
   845→              continue_ := false))
   846→          else continue_ := false
   847→        done;
   848→        !result))
   849→  in
   850→  (* find_point_seg: try trace_segs from hint, fall back to exhaustive scan *)
   851→  if segnum >= 0 && segnum < n_segments
   852→  then (
   853→    let newseg = trace segnum 0 in
   854→    if newseg <> -1
   855→    then newseg
   856→    else if doing_lighting_hack = 0
   857→    then (
   858→      (* Exhaustive scan: find first segment where centermask=0 *)
   859→      let found = ref (-1) in
   860→      let s = ref 0 in
   861→      while !s < n_segments && !found = -1 do
   862→        let _children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions =
   863→          unpack_seg !s
   864→        in
   865→        let _facemask, _sidemask, centermask =
   866→          get_seg_masks
   867→            ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   868→            ~rad:0
   869→            ~seg_verts
   870→            ~side_types
   871→            ~normals
   872→            ~seg_vert_positions
   873→        in
   874→        if centermask = 0 then found := !s;
   875→        incr s
   876→      done;
   877→      !found)
   878→    else -1)
   879→  else if doing_lighting_hack = 0
   880→  then (
   881→    (* segnum=-1: no hint, go straight to exhaustive *)
   882→    let found = ref (-1) in
   883→    let s = ref 0 in
   884→    while !s < n_segments && !found = -1 do
   885→      let _children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = unpack_seg !s in
   886→      let _facemask, _sidemask, centermask =
   887→        get_seg_masks
   888→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   889→          ~rad:0
   890→          ~seg_verts
   891→          ~side_types
   892→          ~normals
   893→          ~seg_vert_positions
   894→      in
   895→      if centermask = 0 then found := !s;
   896→      incr s
   897→    done;
   898→    !found)
   899→  else -1
   900→;;
   901→
   902→(* find_point_seg_v2: same algorithm as find_point_seg but fetches segment
   903→   data on demand via Fetch_segment_data effects instead of a pre-packed array.
   904→   The 87-int segment data uses the same layout as FVI packed segments:
   905→     [0..5] children, [6..11] side_types, [12..19] seg_verts,
   906→     [20..55] normals (side*2, xyz each), [56..79] vert positions (8 verts, xyz each),
   907→     [80..85] wid, [86] first_object.
   908→   We only need the first 80 ints (same as the old find_point_seg format). *)
   909→let unpack_seg_from_fetch (seg_data : int array) =
   910→  let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(i)) in
   911→  let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(6 + i)) in
   912→  let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(12 + i)) in
   913→  let normals =
   914→    Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
   915→      let off = 20 + (i * 3) in
   916→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   917→  in
   918→  let seg_vert_positions =
   919→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   920→      let off = 56 + (i * 3) in
   921→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
   922→  in
   923→  children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions
   924→;;
   925→
   926→let find_point_seg_v2 ~point:(px, py, pz) ~segnum ~n_segments ~doing_lighting_hack =
   927→  let fetch s = Effect.perform (Fetch_segment_data s) |> unpack_seg_from_fetch in
   928→  let rec trace s iterations =
   929→    if s < 0 || s >= n_segments
   930→    then -1
   931→    else if iterations > 1024
   932→    then s
   933→    else (
   934→      let children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = fetch s in
   935→      let centermask, side_dists =
   936→        get_side_dists
   937→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   938→          ~seg_verts
   939→          ~side_types
   940→          ~normals
   941→          ~seg_vert_positions
   942→      in
   943→      if centermask = 0
   944→      then s
   945→      else (
   946→        let sd = Array.copy side_dists in
   947→        let result = ref (-1) in
   948→        let continue_ = ref true in
   949→        while !continue_ do
   950→          let biggest_side = ref (-1) in
   951→          let biggest_val = ref 0 in
   952→          let bit = ref 1 in
   953→          for sn = 0 to 5 do
   954→            if centermask land !bit <> 0 && children.(sn) > -1 && sd.(sn) < !biggest_val
   955→            then (
   956→              biggest_val := sd.(sn);
   957→              biggest_side := sn);
   958→            bit := !bit lsl 1
   959→          done;
   960→          if !biggest_side <> -1
   961→          then (
   962→            sd.(!biggest_side) <- 0;
   963→            let check = trace children.(!biggest_side) (iterations + 1) in
   964→            if check <> -1
   965→            then (
   966→              result := check;
   967→              continue_ := false))
   968→          else continue_ := false
   969→        done;
   970→        !result))
   971→  in
   972→  let exhaustive_scan () =
   973→    let found = ref (-1) in
   974→    let s = ref 0 in
   975→    while !s < n_segments && !found = -1 do
   976→      let _children, side_types, seg_verts, normals, seg_vert_positions = fetch !s in
   977→      let _facemask, _sidemask, centermask =
   978→        get_seg_masks
   979→          ~checkp:(px, py, pz)
   980→          ~rad:0
   981→          ~seg_verts
   982→          ~side_types
   983→          ~normals
   984→          ~seg_vert_positions
   985→      in
   986→      if centermask = 0 then found := !s;
   987→      incr s
   988→    done;
   989→    !found
   990→  in
   991→  if segnum >= 0 && segnum < n_segments
   992→  then (
   993→    let newseg = trace segnum 0 in
   994→    if newseg <> -1
   995→    then newseg
   996→    else if doing_lighting_hack = 0
   997→    then exhaustive_scan ()
   998→    else -1)
   999→  else if doing_lighting_hack = 0
  1000→  then exhaustive_scan ()
  1001→  else -1
  1002→;;
  1003→
  1004→let max_vertices_per_segment = 8
  1005→
  1006→(* pick_random_point_in_seg: pick a random point inside a segment.
  1007→   Computes the segment center, picks a random vertex, then moves
  1008→   a random fraction toward that vertex.
  1009→   C original: gameseg.cpp pick_random_point_in_seg *)
  1010→let pick_random_point_in_seg ~segnum =
  1011→  let seg_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_segment_data segnum) in
  1012→  (* Extract 8 vertex positions from segment data *)
  1013→  let verts =
  1014→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
  1015→      let off = 56 + (i * 3) in
  1016→      seg_data.(off), seg_data.(off + 1), seg_data.(off + 2))
  1017→  in
  1018→  let center =
  1019→    compute_segment_center
  1020→      ~v0:verts.(0) ~v1:verts.(1) ~v2:verts.(2) ~v3:verts.(3)
  1021→      ~v4:verts.(4) ~v5:verts.(5) ~v6:verts.(6) ~v7:verts.(7)
  1022→  in
  1023→  let vnum = (Ox_misc.p_rand () * max_vertices_per_segment) asr 15 in
  1024→  let vec2 = Ox_math.vm_vec_sub ~a:verts.(vnum) ~b:center in
  1025→  let vec2 = Ox_math.vm_vec_scale ~v:vec2 ~k:(Ox_misc.p_rand ()) in
  1026→  Ox_math.vm_vec_add2 ~a:center ~b:vec2
  1027→;;
  1028→
  1029→(* --- check_segment_connections ---------------------------------------- *)
  1030→(* Heavy-duty error checking: validate that segment connections are consistent.
  1031→   For each segment side with a child, checks that the child connects back,
  1032→   vertex lists match (with correct orientation), and face normals are negations.
  1033→   Returns 1 if errors detected, 0 if OK.
  1034→   C original: gameseg.cpp check_segment_connections (debug-only) *)
  1035→
  1036→(* Effect: set a segment side's type (used for debug repair of mismatched triangulation) *)
  1037→type _ Effect.t += Set_segment_side_type : (int * int * int) -> unit Effect.t
  1038→
  1039→let check_segment_connections ~highest_segment_index =
  1040→  let errors = ref 0 in
  1041→  for segnum = 0 to highest_segment_index do
  1042→    let seg_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_segment_data segnum) in
  1043→    let children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(i)) in
  1044→    let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(6 + i)) in
  1045→    let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> seg_data.(12 + i)) in
  1046→    for sidenum = 0 to 5 do
  1047→      let num_faces, vertex_list =
  1048→        create_abs_vertex_lists ~side_type:side_types.(sidenum) ~seg_verts ~sidenum
  1049→      in
  1050→      let csegnum = children.(sidenum) in
  1051→      if csegnum >= 0
  1052→      then (
  1053→        let cseg_data = Effect.perform (Fetch_segment_data csegnum) in
  1054→        let cseg_children = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> cseg_data.(i)) in
  1055→        let cseg_side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> cseg_data.(6 + i)) in
  1056→        let cseg_seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> cseg_data.(12 + i)) in
  1057→        let csidenum =
  1058→          find_connect_side ~children:cseg_children ~base_seg_num:segnum
  1059→        in
  1060→        if csidenum = -1
  1061→        then errors := 1
  1062→        else (
  1063→          let con_num_faces, con_vertex_list =
  1064→            create_abs_vertex_lists
  1065→              ~side_type:cseg_side_types.(csidenum)
  1066→              ~seg_verts:cseg_seg_verts
  1067→              ~sidenum:csidenum
  1068→          in
  1069→          if con_num_faces <> num_faces
  1070→          then errors := 1
  1071→          else if num_faces = 1
  1072→          then (
  1073→            (* Find rotation offset t where con_vertex_list[t] = vertex_list[0] *)
  1074→            let t = ref 0 in
  1075→            while !t < 4 && con_vertex_list.(!t) <> vertex_list.(0) do
  1076→              incr t
  1077→            done;
  1078→            if !t = 4
  1079→               || vertex_list.(0) <> con_vertex_list.(!t)
  1080→               || vertex_list.(1) <> con_vertex_list.((!t + 3) mod 4)
  1081→               || vertex_list.(2) <> con_vertex_list.((!t + 2) mod 4)
  1082→               || vertex_list.(3) <> con_vertex_list.((!t + 1) mod 4)
  1083→            then errors := 1
  1084→            else (
  1085→              (* check_norms(segnum, sidenum, 0, csegnum, csidenum, 0) *)
  1086→              let off0 = 20 + ((sidenum * 2) + 0) * 3 in
  1087→              let n0 = seg_data.(off0), seg_data.(off0 + 1), seg_data.(off0 + 2) in
  1088→              let coff0 = 20 + ((csidenum * 2) + 0) * 3 in
  1089→              let n1 = cseg_data.(coff0), cseg_data.(coff0 + 1), cseg_data.(coff0 + 2) in
  1090→              errors := !errors lor check_norms ~n0 ~n1))
  1091→          else (
  1092→            (* num_faces = 2: triangulated side *)
  1093→            if vertex_list.(1) = con_vertex_list.(1)
  1094→            then (
  1095→              if vertex_list.(4) <> con_vertex_list.(4)
  1096→                 || vertex_list.(0) <> con_vertex_list.(2)
  1097→                 || vertex_list.(2) <> con_vertex_list.(0)
  1098→                 || vertex_list.(3) <> con_vertex_list.(5)
  1099→                 || vertex_list.(5) <> con_vertex_list.(3)
  1100→              then (
  1101→                (* Flip the connected side's type: type = 5 - type *)
  1102→                let new_type = 5 - cseg_side_types.(csidenum) in
  1103→                Effect.perform (Set_segment_side_type (csegnum, csidenum, new_type)))
  1104→              else (
  1105→                (* check_norms face 0 vs 0, face 1 vs 1 *)
  1106→                let off0 = 20 + ((sidenum * 2) + 0) * 3 in
  1107→                let n0_0 =
  1108→                  seg_data.(off0), seg_data.(off0 + 1), seg_data.(off0 + 2)
  1109→                in
  1110→                let coff0 = 20 + ((csidenum * 2) + 0) * 3 in
  1111→                let n1_0 =
  1112→                  cseg_data.(coff0), cseg_data.(coff0 + 1), cseg_data.(coff0 + 2)
  1113→                in
  1114→                errors := !errors lor check_norms ~n0:n0_0 ~n1:n1_0;
  1115→                let off1 = 20 + ((sidenum * 2) + 1) * 3 in
  1116→                let n0_1 =
  1117→                  seg_data.(off1), seg_data.(off1 + 1), seg_data.(off1 + 2)
  1118→                in
  1119→                let coff1 = 20 + ((csidenum * 2) + 1) * 3 in
  1120→                let n1_1 =
  1121→                  cseg_data.(coff1), cseg_data.(coff1 + 1), cseg_data.(coff1 + 2)
  1122→                in
  1123→                errors := !errors lor check_norms ~n0:n0_1 ~n1:n1_1))
  1124→            else if vertex_list.(1) <> con_vertex_list.(4)
  1125→                    || vertex_list.(4) <> con_vertex_list.(1)
  1126→                    || vertex_list.(0) <> con_vertex_list.(5)
  1127→                    || vertex_list.(5) <> con_vertex_list.(0)
  1128→                    || vertex_list.(2) <> con_vertex_list.(3)
  1129→                    || vertex_list.(3) <> con_vertex_list.(2)
  1130→            then (
  1131→              (* Flip the connected side's type: type = 5 - type *)
  1132→              let new_type = 5 - cseg_side_types.(csidenum) in
  1133→              Effect.perform (Set_segment_side_type (csegnum, csidenum, new_type)))
  1134→            else (
  1135→              (* check_norms face 0 vs 1, face 1 vs 0 *)
  1136→              let off0 = 20 + ((sidenum * 2) + 0) * 3 in
  1137→              let n0_0 =
  1138→                seg_data.(off0), seg_data.(off0 + 1), seg_data.(off0 + 2)
  1139→              in
  1140→              let coff1 = 20 + ((csidenum * 2) + 1) * 3 in
  1141→              let n1_1 =
  1142→                cseg_data.(coff1), cseg_data.(coff1 + 1), cseg_data.(coff1 + 2)
  1143→              in
  1144→              errors := !errors lor check_norms ~n0:n0_0 ~n1:n1_1;
  1145→              let off1 = 20 + ((sidenum * 2) + 1) * 3 in
  1146→              let n0_1 =
  1147→                seg_data.(off1), seg_data.(off1 + 1), seg_data.(off1 + 2)
  1148→              in
  1149→              let coff0 = 20 + ((csidenum * 2) + 0) * 3 in
  1150→              let n1_0 =
  1151→                cseg_data.(coff0), cseg_data.(coff0 + 1), cseg_data.(coff0 + 2)
  1152→              in
  1153→              errors := !errors lor check_norms ~n0:n0_1 ~n1:n1_0))))
  1154→    done
  1155→  done;
  1156→  !errors
  1157→;;
  1158→
  1159→(* --- validate_removable_wall ------------------------------------------ *)
  1160→(* Calls create_walls_on_side and passes through tmap_num unchanged.
  1161→   Returns: (side_type, n0, n1, tmap_num) *)
  1162→let validate_removable_wall ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child ~tmap_num =
  1163→  let side_type, n0, n1 =
  1164→    create_walls_on_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child
  1165→  in
  1166→  side_type, n0, n1, tmap_num
  1167→;;
  1168→
  1169→(* --- validate_segment_side --------------------------------------------- *)
  1170→(* Dispatcher: if wall_num == -1, call create_walls_on_side;
  1171→   otherwise call validate_removable_wall.
  1172→   Returns: (side_type, n0, n1, tmap_num) — tmap_num passed through unchanged
  1173→   when wall_num == -1. *)
  1174→let validate_segment_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child ~wall_num ~tmap_num =
  1175→  if wall_num = -1 then
  1176→    let side_type, n0, n1 =
  1177→      create_walls_on_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child
  1178→    in
  1179→    (side_type, n0, n1, tmap_num)
  1180→  else
  1181→    validate_removable_wall ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child ~tmap_num
  1182→;;
  1183→

1→let cd_compute_center_point_on_side v0x v0y v0z v1x v1y v1z v2x v2y v2z v3x v3y v3z =
     2→  Ox_gameseg.compute_center_point_on_side
     3→    ~v0:(v0x, v0y, v0z)
     4→    ~v1:(v1x, v1y, v1z)
     5→    ~v2:(v2x, v2y, v2z)
     6→    ~v3:(v3x, v3y, v3z)
     7→;;
     8→
     9→let cd_compute_segment_center (arr : int array) =
    10→  let v i = arr.(i * 3), arr.((i * 3) + 1), arr.((i * 3) + 2) in
    11→  let cx, cy, cz =
    12→    Ox_gameseg.compute_segment_center
    13→      ~v0:(v 0)
    14→      ~v1:(v 1)
    15→      ~v2:(v 2)
    16→      ~v3:(v 3)
    17→      ~v4:(v 4)
    18→      ~v5:(v 5)
    19→      ~v6:(v 6)
    20→      ~v7:(v 7)
    21→  in
    22→  [| cx; cy; cz |]
    23→;;
    24→
    25→let cd_get_verts_for_normal va vb vc vd =
    26→  let v0, v1, v2, v3, negate_flag = Ox_gameseg.get_verts_for_normal ~va ~vb ~vc ~vd in
    27→  v0, v1, v2, v3, negate_flag
    28→;;
    29→
    30→let cd_create_abs_vertex_lists side_type (seg_verts : int array) sidenum =
    31→  let num_faces, vertices =
    32→    Ox_gameseg.create_abs_vertex_lists ~side_type ~seg_verts ~sidenum
    33→  in
    34→  let result = Array.create ~len:7 0 in
    35→  result.(0) <- num_faces;
    36→  for i = 0 to 5 do
    37→    result.(i + 1) <- vertices.(i)
    38→  done;
    39→  result
    40→;;
    41→
    42→(* Pack format for get_seg_masks:
    43→   [0..2]  = checkp (x,y,z)
    44→   [3]     = rad
    45→   [4..11] = seg_verts[0..7] (absolute vertex indices)
    46→   [12..17] = side_types[0..5]
    47→   [18..53] = normals[0..11] as 12 * (x,y,z)
    48→   [54..77] = seg_vert_positions[0..7] as 8 * (x,y,z) *)
    49→let cd_get_seg_masks (arr : int array) =
    50→  let cpx = arr.(0) in
    51→  let cpy = arr.(1) in
    52→  let cpz = arr.(2) in
    53→  let rad = arr.(3) in
    54→  let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(4 + i)) in
    55→  let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(12 + i)) in
    56→  let normals =
    57→    Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
    58→      let base = 18 + (i * 3) in
    59→      arr.(base), arr.(base + 1), arr.(base + 2))
    60→  in
    61→  let seg_vert_positions =
    62→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
    63→      let base = 54 + (i * 3) in
    64→      arr.(base), arr.(base + 1), arr.(base + 2))
    65→  in
    66→  let facemask, sidemask, centermask =
    67→    Ox_gameseg.get_seg_masks
    68→      ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz)
    69→      ~rad
    70→      ~seg_verts
    71→      ~side_types
    72→      ~normals
    73→      ~seg_vert_positions
    74→  in
    75→  [| facemask; sidemask; centermask |]
    76→;;
    77→
    78→(* Pack format for get_side_dists:
    79→   [0..2]  = checkp (x,y,z)
    80→   [3..10] = seg_verts[0..7]
    81→   [11..16] = side_types[0..5]
    82→   [17..52] = normals[0..11] as 12 * (x,y,z)
    83→   [53..76] = seg_vert_positions[0..7] as 8 * (x,y,z) *)
    84→let cd_get_side_dists (arr : int array) =
    85→  let cpx = arr.(0) in
    86→  let cpy = arr.(1) in
    87→  let cpz = arr.(2) in
    88→  let seg_verts = Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(3 + i)) in
    89→  let side_types = Array.init 6 ~f:(fun i -> arr.(11 + i)) in
    90→  let normals =
    91→    Array.init 12 ~f:(fun i ->
    92→      let base = 17 + (i * 3) in
    93→      arr.(base), arr.(base + 1), arr.(base + 2))
    94→  in
    95→  let seg_vert_positions =
    96→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
    97→      let base = 53 + (i * 3) in
    98→      arr.(base), arr.(base + 1), arr.(base + 2))
    99→  in
   100→  let mask, side_dists =
   101→    Ox_gameseg.get_side_dists
   102→      ~checkp:(cpx, cpy, cpz)
   103→      ~seg_verts
   104→      ~side_types
   105→      ~normals
   106→      ~seg_vert_positions
   107→  in
   108→  let result = Array.create ~len:7 0 in
   109→  result.(0) <- mask;
   110→  for i = 0 to 5 do
   111→    result.(i + 1) <- side_dists.(i)
   112→  done;
   113→  result
   114→;;

   116→let cd_extract_vector_from_segment (arr : int array) =
   117→  let verts =
   118→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   119→      let base = i * 3 in
   120→      arr.(base), arr.(base + 1), arr.(base + 2))
   121→  in
   122→  let start_side = arr.(24) in
   123→  let end_side = arr.(25) in
   124→  let vx, vy, vz = Ox_gameseg.extract_vector_from_segment ~verts ~start_side ~end_side in
   125→  [| vx; vy; vz |]
   126→;;
   127→
   128→let cd_extract_orient_from_segment (arr : int array) =
   129→  let verts =
   130→    Array.init 8 ~f:(fun i ->
   131→      let base = i * 3 in
   132→      arr.(base), arr.(base + 1), arr.(base + 2))
   133→  in
   134→  let (r1, r2, r3), (u1, u2, u3), (f1, f2, f3) =
   135→    Ox_gameseg.extract_orient_from_segment verts
   136→  in
   137→  [| r1; r2; r3; u1; u2; u3; f1; f2; f3 |]

   140→(* find_connect_side: 7 scalar args → 1 int
   141→   children(6) + base_seg_num(1) = 7 in
   142→   Returns: side index or -1 *)
   143→let cd_find_connect_side c0 c1 c2 c3 c4 c5 base_seg_num =
   144→  let children = [| c0; c1; c2; c3; c4; c5 |] in
   145→  Ox_gameseg.find_connect_side ~children ~base_seg_num

   147→
   148→(* create_shortpos: packed int array → int array (17 ints)
   149→   Input: [orient(9), pos(3), vel(3), seg_vert0(3), segnum] = 19 ints
   150→   Output: [bytemat(9), xo, yo, zo, segment, velx, vely, velz] = 17 ints *)
   151→let cd_create_shortpos (packed : int array) =
   152→  let orient = Array.init 9 ~f:(fun i -> packed.(i)) in
   153→  let pos = packed.(9), packed.(10), packed.(11) in
   154→  let vel = packed.(12), packed.(13), packed.(14) in
   155→  let seg_vert0 = packed.(15), packed.(16), packed.(17) in
   156→  let segnum = packed.(18) in
   157→  let bytemat, xo, yo, zo, segment, velx, vely, velz =
   158→    Ox_gameseg.create_shortpos ~orient ~pos ~vel ~seg_vert0 ~segnum
   159→  in
   160→  let buf = Array.create ~len:17 0 in
   161→  Array.blit ~src:bytemat ~dst:buf ~src_pos:0 ~dst_pos:0 ~len:9;
   162→  buf.(9) <- xo;
   163→  buf.(10) <- yo;
   164→  buf.(11) <- zo;
   165→  buf.(12) <- segment;
   166→  buf.(13) <- velx;
   167→  buf.(14) <- vely;
   168→  buf.(15) <- velz;
   169→  buf.(16) <- Array.length bytemat;
   170→  buf
   171→;;
   172→
   173→(* extract_shortpos: packed int array → int array (15 ints)
   174→   Input: [bytemat(9), xo, yo, zo, seg_vert0(3), velx, vely, velz] = 18 ints
   175→   Output: [orient(9), pos(3), vel(3)] = 15 ints *)
   176→let cd_extract_shortpos (packed : int array) =
   177→  let bytemat = Array.init 9 ~f:(fun i -> packed.(i)) in
   178→  let xo = packed.(9) in
   179→  let yo = packed.(10) in
   180→  let zo = packed.(11) in
   181→  let seg_vert0 = packed.(12), packed.(13), packed.(14) in
   182→  let velx = packed.(15) in
   183→  let vely = packed.(16) in
   184→  let velz = packed.(17) in
   185→  let orient, (px, py, pz), (vx, vy, vz) =
   186→    Ox_gameseg.extract_shortpos ~bytemat ~xo ~yo ~zo ~seg_vert0 ~velx ~vely ~velz
   187→  in
   188→  let buf = Array.create ~len:15 0 in
   189→  Array.blit ~src:orient ~dst:buf ~src_pos:0 ~dst_pos:0 ~len:9;
   190→  buf.(9) <- px;
   191→  buf.(10) <- py;
   192→  buf.(11) <- pz;
   193→  buf.(12) <- vx;
   194→  buf.(13) <- vy;
   195→  buf.(14) <- vz;
   196→  buf
   197→;;
   198→
   199→(* create_walls_on_side: packed int array → int array (7 ints)
   200→   Input: [v0(3), v1(3), v2(3), v3(3), vi0, vi1, vi2, vi3, has_child] = 17 ints
   201→   Output: [side_type, n0x, n0y, n0z, n1x, n1y, n1z] = 7 ints *)
   202→let cd_create_walls_on_side (packed : int array) =
   203→  let v0 = packed.(0), packed.(1), packed.(2) in
   204→  let v1 = packed.(3), packed.(4), packed.(5) in
   205→  let v2 = packed.(6), packed.(7), packed.(8) in
   206→  let v3 = packed.(9), packed.(10), packed.(11) in
   207→  let vi0 = packed.(12) in
   208→  let vi1 = packed.(13) in
   209→  let vi2 = packed.(14) in
   210→  let vi3 = packed.(15) in
   211→  let has_child = packed.(16) <> 0 in
   212→  let side_type, (n0x, n0y, n0z), (n1x, n1y, n1z) =
   213→    Ox_gameseg.create_walls_on_side ~v0 ~v1 ~v2 ~v3 ~vi0 ~vi1 ~vi2 ~vi3 ~has_child
   214→  in
   215→  [| side_type; n0x; n0y; n0z; n1x; n1y; n1z |]
   216→;;
   217→
   218→(* check_norms: 6 scalar ints → 1 int *)
   219→let cd_check_norms n0x n0y n0z n1x n1y n1z =
   220→  Ox_gameseg.check_norms ~n0:(n0x, n0y, n0z) ~n1:(n1x, n1y, n1z)
   221→;;

   223→(* create_all_vertex_lists: side_type + sidenum → packed 7 ints
   224→   Output: [num_faces, vertices(6)] *)
   225→let cd_create_all_vertex_lists side_type sidenum =
   226→  let num_faces, vertices = Ox_gameseg.create_all_vertex_lists ~side_type ~sidenum in
   227→  let buf = Array.create ~len:7 0 in

   233→(* create_all_vertnum_lists: side_type → packed 7 ints

   235→let cd_create_all_vertnum_lists side_type =
